description_long: This is a monumental equestrian statue featuring a rider atop a horse, both rendered in a greenish patina suggestive of aged bronze. The statue is mounted on a multi-tiered stone pedestal, which is square in plan and rises in several steps, each layer slightly smaller than the one below. Four ornate posts, possibly lanterns or decorative columns, are positioned at each corner of the base, adding to the grandeur and symmetry of the structure. The pedestal includes relief panels on its sides, likely depicting historical or commemorative scenes. The entire monument sits on a broad, square stone platform bordered by a reddish-brown trim. The overall appearance is stately and commemorative, typical of public monuments found in plazas or parks.
description: A grand equestrian statue of a rider on horseback atop a multi-tiered stone pedestal, flanked by four decorative posts and set on a broad square platform. The statue features a greenish patina and detailed relief panels on the pedestal.
description_view_0: The front view shows the rider and horse in profile, with the left side of the horse visible and the detailed relief panel on the pedestal clearly seen.
description_view_1: This view presents the statue head-on, with the rider facing forward and the four corner posts symmetrically framing the monument.
description_view_2: The rear profile of the horse and rider is visible, with the right side of the horse and another relief panel on the pedestal in view.
description_view_3: The back view shows the rider from behind, with the horse's tail and the rear of the pedestal and platform visible.
attribute_subject_matter: equestrian statue of a historical figure
attribute_location_context: Likely installed in an outdoor urban plaza or park, as suggested by the monumental base and open platform.
